compiled_state.title: [ticket-platform-compat tests / macos-bash3]: 15/17 tests assert empty result -> non-empty -> 'expected: non-empty, actual: empty' on macOS Bash 3.2 leg
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Sub-Agent Blocker (surfaced by 9cb3-6ef0 fix; previously hidden by workflow startup_failure)

#### Expected Behavior

`bash tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh` should pass under macOS bash 3.2.x (matrix leg `macos-bash3`) â€” round-trip tests C1â€“C17 covering special-char/unicode/newline handling in ticket titles, comment bodies, and tags.

#### Actual Behavior

Run https://github.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra/actions/runs/25027977388/job/73303271806 produces 15 FAIL / 2 PASS. Failure pattern is uniform: assertion fires on `expected: non-empty, actual: empty` for either "C{N}: created ticket" or "C{N}: ticket_create returned id". Examples:

```
Test C4: embedded newlines in comment body round-trip
FAIL: C4: created ticket
  expected: non-empty
  actual:   empty
```

The same suite passes on `linux-bash4` (Ubuntu bash 5.x). The platform delta appears to be in `ticket_create` returning an empty ID under bash 3.2 (macOS default), suggesting the create path uses a bash-4-only feature (associative arrays, `mapfile`, `${var^^}`/`${var,,}`, `BASH_REMATCH` semantics, etc.) silently producing empty stdout instead of erroring.

### 1. Technical Environment

- Workflow: `.github/workflows/ticket-platform-matrix.yml` matrix leg `macos-bash3` (`macos-latest`, native bash 3.2)
- Tests file: `tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh`
- Library under test: `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh`
- Surfaced on: PR #10 (https://github.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra/pull/10)

### 4. Chronological Rationalization

This bug was hidden for 5+ days because the workflow had a separate startup_failure (bug 9cb3-6ef0) preventing any leg from executing. Fixing the startup_failure surfaced the platform-compat regression. The most likely categories: (a) bash 4+ associative arrays in `ticket_create`, (b) `mapfile`/`readarray` usage, (c) `${var,,}` lowercase syntax, (d) macOS BSD vs GNU coreutils divergence in `mktemp`/`sed`/`grep` behavior.

### 6. Logs

Excerpt from job 73303271806 (full log via `gh run view --job 73303271806 --log-failed`):

```
PASSED: 2  FAILED: 15
##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.
```

### Suggested Investigation

1. Run `tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh` locally on macOS bash 3.2 (`/bin/bash --version` to confirm).
2. Add `set -x` to `ticket_create` and one of the failing C-tests to identify which line returns empty.
3. Search `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` for bash-4 idioms: `declare -A`, `mapfile`, `readarray`, `${var,,}`, `${var^^}`, `&>>`.
